free_text: New measurements and evaluation of database for
deuteron induced reaction on Ni up to 50 MeV
Experiments 1 to 3 are earlier reported works where
Ni foils were used as backing or energy degrader:
Exp.1
Exp.2
Exp.3
Ep=50 MeV irradiation
Ep=20 MeV irradiation
Thin foils of high purity Ni (Exp.4) or Ga-Ni (70-30%)
alloy (Exp.5).
Ga-Ni alloy (70-30%) was used as primary target
material (nominal thickness 17 um) electroplated onto
Au backing and also pure Ni foils were inserted into
the stack and irradiated with 50 MeV deuteron beam.
Five stacked foil experiments in which Ni
was included either as beam energy degrading foils
together with other materials, or as backing material
for electrodeposition of different other target
materials or part of an alloy.
Al and Ti monitor foils were inserted into the
stack.
Activity was measured without chemical
separation. The decay data were taken from the online
NuDat2. database.
Uncertainty on stopping in each foil due to
thickness and straggling effects.
Square root of the quadratic summation of the
independently contributing parameters.
Surface density of target nuclei     (5%)
Incident charged particle flux       (6%)
Decay data                   (1-5%)
Detector efficiency          (3-8%)
Statistical and peak fitting (3-8%)
Integral physical yields derived from fitted
excitation functions
On. Data revised in 040-041.
VS: SF4-5 corrected in 018,030,051,055.2
